name: Webflow MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Webflow — the visual web design and CMS platform. Enables AI agents to interact with Webflow sites via the Data API — managing CMS collections and items, querying site content, updating pages, and managing assets. Allows content automation and AI-driven site management.
use_cases: Managing Webflow CMS content and collections from content automation agents
Publishing and updating site content from editorial workflow agents
Querying Webflow site data for content analytics agents
Building AI-powered content management pipelines for Webflow-hosted sites
not_for: Visual design changes (Webflow Designer is UI-only, not accessible via API)
High-frequency content publishing at scale (rate limits apply)
best_when: A content team using Webflow as their CMS wants AI agents to automate content operations — creating, updating, and publishing CMS items without manual Webflow dashboard work.
avoid_when: You need to make visual design changes — those require the Webflow Designer UI. Also: Webflow CMS has item limits by plan — verify your plan supports your content volume.

auth.notes: Webflow OAuth 2.0 or API token. Site-level tokens for specific site access. OAuth for multi-site integrations. Use CMS:write scope only when needed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Webflow Starter free for 2 sites; CMS API requires paid CMS plan
pricing.notes: Webflow CMS API requires CMS or higher plan. MCP server is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Webflow API: 60 requests/min for Data API

known_agent_gotchas: Visual design changes require Webflow Designer — API is for CMS/data only
CMS item publishing requires explicit publish step — draft vs published states
Webflow API v2 vs legacy v1 — ensure using current API version
Rate limits (60 req/min) may be restrictive for bulk content operations
CMS slug uniqueness constraints can cause issues with automated content creation
agent_readiness.security_notes: Website content management. Published content is public. CMS data may include drafts and sensitive content. OAuth minimal scopes. SOC2 certified.

security_score_components.security_notes: CMS content includes drafts that may be sensitive. Published content is public. OAuth with minimal scopes. Site token per-site isolation. SOC2.
